warnings: Terminal target successful_landing has low step evidence but episode semantic evidence is present; warning only.

reflection_strategy.why_atomic_or_separable: Reducing r_progress weight without increasing the terminal landing bonus would create a sparser reward signal that may severely slow or prevent learning. The two changes must be applied together to maintain overall signal sufficiency.
editor_constraints: Change only the 'weight' field of components/event rules; do not modify formulas or conditions.
All other weights remain at their current values.
must_preserve: The condition and one‑time flag of successful_landing.
must_avoid: Changing any formula, condition, or adding new components/event rules.
Setting r_progress weight to zero (risk of total shaping loss).
Increasing the weight of the crash event.
Making more than 2 parameter changes in one package.
reflection_strategy.expected_effect: r_progress dominance should drop below 50%, allowing r_stability and r_control_cost to influence behaviour more; the strong terminal incentive should push the agent to complete a landing rather than hover, raising semantic success rate.
reflection_strategy.risk_analysis: If successful_landing weight is too high, early exploration may be insufficient because the terminal bonus is rarely seen; the reduced r_progress weight may then cause initial performance to degrade. The chosen ranges attempt to balance these risks. There is also a small chance the agent will learn a new form of progress hacking (e.g., fast descent to trigger terminal event) – the unchanged stability and control components should help penalise such behaviour.
